title: Alyssum cretaceum

This family, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family, is a large group of flowering plants. The classification of Alyssum cretaceum is further divided into more specific taxonomic groups, including genus, species, and other ranks. intro: Alyssum cretaceum, also known as Cretan madwort, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Boraginaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Alyssum cretaceum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Alyssum cretaceum is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and capsules to support respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
